title: Touch targets must meet minimum size requirements
summary: AI-generated mobile interfaces often have touch targets smaller than the recommended minimum, causing user frustration.
instruction: Ensure every interactive element has a touch target of at least 44x44 CSS pixels. Do not rely on the visible element size alone - the interactive area (padding + visible area) must meet the minimum.
rationale: Apple HIG and Material Design both recommend 44-48px minimum touch targets. Users with larger fingers, reduced dexterity, or using the device one-handed need generous tap areas. Small targets increase error rates and user frustration disproportionately.
violations: A mobile navigation with 24px-tall links that are densely packed with 4px gap between them.
A filter bar with 20x20px checkbox controls and small text labels where the tap zone is the checkbox alone.
Icon buttons (edit, delete, share) at 28x28px without padding extending the hit area to 44px.
controls: Mobile navigation links with 48px height and at least 8px gap between interactive rows.
Filter controls with 44x44px touch targets using transparent padding around the visible checkbox and label association.
Icon buttons with visible icon at 24x24px but total touch target expanded to 44x44px through padding.
borderlineCases: Interactive elements within a scrollable data table where increasing row height to 44px significantly reduces data density - may need careful tradeoff.
contextualExceptions: Interactive elements in a packed toolbar where the primary controls meet 44px and secondary controls are smaller but rarely used.
Map interfaces where the map pins themselves are small but respond to nearby taps.
detectorSignal: Interactive elements (buttons, links, inputs) with computed touch target area smaller than 44x44 CSS pixels
matcherProposal: For interactive elements (a, button, input, select, [role=button]), compute the total area including padding. Check if width >= 44px AND height >= 44px. Flag any interactive element below either threshold. Exclude inline text links within paragraphs.
detectorLimitations: Cannot detect touch targets created via JavaScript (custom gestures, SVG hit areas).
font-size-based targets may be responsive and change size.
Overlapping elements may have effective touch areas that differ from computed box model.
falsePositiveScenarios: Desktop-only interactive maps where touch is not the primary input.
Responsive interfaces that increase touch target size through media queries (detect at mobile breakpoints).
Interactive elements inside scrollable containers where the parent handles touch.
mitigationStrategy: Apply only at mobile viewport breakpoints (<768px). Exclude inline text links. Allow suppression for data-dense interfaces with documented tradeoff.

fixturePlans.violation: A mobile form with 32px submit button, 28px icon buttons, 24px navigation links.
fixturePlans.control: Same mobile form with 48px submit button, 44px icon buttons via padding, 44px navigation links.
fixturePlans.borderline: A mobile data table with 36px row heights and touch-friendly checkboxes (44px) at the start of each row.

cleanRoomAttestations: Apple HIG (44px), Material Design (48px), WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (proposed).
overlapNotes: Distinct from existing rules. No mobile-specific rule exists in the catalog.
